samples.claude_rationale: Citi reported solid Q1 2024 results with 3%+ revenue growth ex-divestitures, 35% investment banking growth, strong services momentum, and improving capital ratios, though offset by elevated expenses, restructuring charges, and still-below-target RoTCE of 7.6%.

AMD reported record data center revenue, raised 2024 AI GPU guidance from $2B to $3.5B+, and highlighted strong MI300X ramp and EPYC share gains, with only gaming and embedded declines tempering the broadly bullish tone.

While Intel showed sequential revenue growth and progress on cost cuts, massive impairment charges, a $0.46 EPS loss, missed Gaudi targets, and ongoing foundry losses reflect significant structural challenges despite cautiously improving underlying trends.

The transcript highlights record revenues, EPS, AUM, alternatives fundraising, dominant M&A market share, and strong forward pipeline across all major business lines, with only mild caution noted around market cycles and AI exuberance.

Goldman Sachs reported strong Q1 results with record equities revenues, record AUS of $3.2 trillion, growing financing revenues, and rising M&A backlog, though tempered by macro uncertainty, recession risk, muted investment banking activity, and caution about Q2 conditions post-April 2.

Citi reported strong 2024 results with net income up ~40%, record equities revenue, $20B buyback authorization, and broad business growth, but tempered sentiment with a lowered 2026 RoTCE target of 10-11% and elevated transformation/technology investment needs.

BlackRock reported record AUM of $11.6 trillion, record net inflows of $641 billion, double-digit revenue/operating income/EPS growth, 280 bps margin expansion, and 7% annualized organic base fee growth in Q4—the highest in three years—with management expressing strong optimism about 2025 growth trajectory from GIP, HPS, and Preqin integrations.

BlackRock reported record AUM of $12.5 trillion, double-digit revenue/EPS growth, 6% organic base fee growth for the fourth consecutive quarter above 5%, strong ETF inflows, successful GIP V close above target, and HPS closing adding significant revenue, with management expressing high confidence in continued durable growth.

NVIDIA reported record Q3 revenue of $35.1B (+94% YoY), record Data Center revenue of $30.8B (+112% YoY), Blackwell in full production with staggering demand, Q4 guidance of $37.5B above prior estimates, and management expressed strong confidence across all segments and multiple AI scaling vectors.

AMD reported record Q4 and full-year revenue, net income, and free cash flow with 34% annual growth, accelerating Data Center AI momentum, strong EPYC share gains, and confident multi-year guidance of >35% CAGR and $20+ EPS, reflecting a highly bullish outlook across all segments.

AMD reported record revenue, near-doubled Data Center revenue, strong share gains across CPU and AI segments, accelerated MI350/MI400 roadmaps, and guided for strong double-digit revenue and EPS growth in 2025, with only minor headwinds in Gaming and Embedded.

BlackRock reported record $221B quarterly net inflows, record revenue and operating income, 350bps margin expansion, and 5% organic base fee growth, with strong forward-looking momentum from GIP, Aladdin, iShares, and private markets initiatives.

FedEx reported solid Q4 results with adjusted operating income up 8% and margin expansion, $4B in cumulative cost savings achieved, and clear FY26 targets, but faces headwinds from weak B2B demand, USPS contract expiration, trade policy volatility, and only flat-to-2% Q1 revenue growth guidance.

Goldman Sachs reported solid Q2 results with strong year-over-year revenue growth, record AUS of $2.9 trillion, record financing revenues, rising IB backlog, increased dividend, and bullish commentary on M&A recovery and alternatives fundraising, partially offset by SCB capital buffer concerns and moderating buybacks guidance.

Management highlighted solid earnings growth, 15% fee revenue increase, $25B capital returned, strong credit discipline, and optimism about 2025 growth opportunities, though NII declined year-over-year and loan demand remained weak.
